overview.description: Similarity search and multimedia retrieval research group at Faculty of Mathematics and Physics, Charles University Prague
overview.description_long: SIRET (SImilarity RETrieval) Research Group (SRG) was founded in 2006 at the Department of Software Engineering, Faculty of Mathematics and Physics, Charles University in Prague. The group deals with database methods for efficient and effective similarity search in databases of complex unstructured objects. Research spans three main areas: general methods of indexing similarity in metric and nonmetric spaces, biological applications of similarity search (bioinformatics, structural bioinformatics, cheminformatics), and indexing image/video databases for content-based retrieval. The group is internationally recognised for its video retrieval systems (SOMHunter), winning first place at the Video Browser Showdown in both 2018 and 2020.

history.origin_story: Founded in 2006 at the Department of Software Engineering, MFF UK, by Tomáš Skopal to investigate general methods of similarity search in metric and nonmetric spaces, with applications in multimedia retrieval and bioinformatics.

key_milestones.event: SIRET Research Group founded at MFF UK, Charles University Prague
Co-organized SISAP 2009 (International Conference on Similarity Search and Applications)
1st place at Video Browser Showdown 2018 (Bangkok, MMM conference)
Tutorial at ACM MM (Core A*) in Seoul
Featured on Czech Television for interactive video search system
1st place at Video Browser Showdown 2020 with SOMHunter (Daejeon, MMM conference)
Published R2DT framework for RNA secondary structure prediction (Nature Methods)

financials.parent_company: Charles University, Faculty of Mathematics and Physics

awards: 1st place at Video Browser Showdown 2018 (MMM, Bangkok)
1st place at Video Browser Showdown 2020 (MMM, Daejeon)
partnerships: Department of Software Engineering, MFF UK (host department)
GAČR (Czech Science Foundation) — multiple funded projects
GAUK (Charles University Grant Agency) — multiple funded projects

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group whose primary mission is to advance the science of similarity search; not a commercial product. Their foundational work on indexing algorithms, bioinformatics tools, and multimedia retrieval remains relevant regardless of foundation model progress.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Research group embedded in an academic department; not subject to commercial market dynamics. Funding depends on Czech and EU grant bodies (GAČR, GAUK).
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Part of Charles University's Faculty of Mathematics and Physics with institutional backing. The group has maintained continuous GAČR and GAUK grant funding since 2006 across 10+ funded projects, demonstrating a stable track record of attracting public research funding.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Long-established (since 2006) academic research group with consistent grant funding, internationally recognised publications and competition wins (Video Browser Showdown 2018, 2020), and stable institutional home at Charles University Prague.

metadata.notes: SIRET = SImilarity RETrieval. Official website http://siret.ms.mff.cuni.cz/ is currently inaccessible (connection timeout) but has 135+ Wayback Machine snapshots up to Oct 2024. Address: Malostranské nám. 25, 118 00 Prague, CZ. Email: info@siret.cz. Phone: +420 95155 4227. The group is part of the Katedra softwarového inženýrství (KSI) at MFF UK. Data sourced from Wayback Machine snapshot dated 2024-10-06 (homepage) and 2024-08-15 (members page).
